overview.Summary: The threat actor C0033, motivated by financial gain and possessing basic skills, aims to compromise water and wastewater systems through a complex attack chain involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shared DB or directory, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Web, Vulnerability exploit, Malware, Session hijacking, Brute-force attack, Credential compromise

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 538 - Name: Open-Source Library Manipulation
stage.description: The threat actor gathers information about potential targets and their systems by searching open-source platforms and websites related to water and wastewater systems.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The threat actor develops a phishing campaign targeting employees of water and wastewater systems, aiming to trick them into revealing sensitive information.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-22893 [CWE-22] : Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor sends spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments to employees of water and wastewater systems, exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server to gain initial access to the target's system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-31196 [CWE-79] : Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 579 - Name: Replace Winlogon Helper DLL
stage.description: The threat actor executes a PowerShell script on the compromised system to download and run a payload, taking advantage of a vulnerability in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint to elevate privileges.

Scenario.summary: The threat actor successfully compromises water and wastewater systems by chaining together various techniques, ultimately leading to data exfiltration and deployment of ransomware, causing significant impact on the targeted organizations.
